checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=4612004591.18 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=12742165754.43 (Q1 2016) implies ~36.2% margin; GrossProfit=4586791782.23 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=12967141588.15 (Q2 2018) implies ~35.4% margin

InventoryNet=5205475324.95 (2016-12-31) vs InventoryNet=5459542861.30 (2017-12-30), a modest 4.9% increase against roughly flat annual revenue (SalesRevenueGoodsNet=51370128514.11 FY2016 vs 50875569569.30 FY2017)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-771899843.27 (Q4 2014, quarterly) and NetIncomeLoss=-318069282.15 (Q2 2015, quarterly) and NetIncomeLoss=-238551961.61 (Q3 2015, quarterly) followed by NetIncomeLoss=15521393079.56 (Q4 2017, quarterly)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: No further AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent values disclosed after 2016-12-31, despite AccountsReceivableNetCurrent nearly doubling to 3781921342.64 by 2018-06-30
evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet, period 2018-06-30 (allowance concept absent from filing)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-Q/A filed 2017-11-07 (accession 0001637459-17-000116) for period 2017-04-01, and 10-Q/A filed 2017-11-07 (accession 0001637459-17-000117) for period 2017-07-01, filed the same day as the original 10-Q (accession 0001637459-17-000118) for period 2017-09-30

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Revenues concept series ends at Revenues=9107642371.81 (2014-09-28 to 2014-12-27); later periods only report SalesRevenueGoodsNet, breaking concept continuity

Assets=44504486692.09 (end 2014-12-27, filed 2015-08-10) versus Assets=70927510472.67 (end 2014-12-28, filed 2016-03-03) for essentially the same fiscal year-end, indicating a change in reporting entity/basis around the 2015 merger that complicates period-over-period comparisons

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature revenue recognition, extended/aggressive customer credit terms, or under-reserved receivables causing net accounts receivable to nearly double (87% QoQ) while revenue grew only ~6% in the same quarter; combined with the disappearance of allowance-for-doubtful-accounts disclosure after 2016, this pattern is consistent with receivables being recorded faster than cash-collectible sales activity, inflating current assets and possibly revenue in the most recent quarter before the cutoff.

affected_line_items: Unspecified restated line items in Q1/Q2 2017 10-Q filings
mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Filing of two 10-Q/A amendments for Q1 2017 and Q2 2017 concurrently with the original Q3 2017 10-Q suggests a correction of previously issued interim financial statements, indicating that reported figures for those quarters were initially misstated and subsequently restated - a classic marker of financial reporting quality issues even though the specific corrected line items are not itemized in the provided data.

FY2017 net income of ~$21.3B (versus $7.0B in FY2016) was driven largely by a non-cash item (consistent with a one-time deferred tax remeasurement benefit around year-end 2017), while operating cash flow was only ~$1.0B for the year - a ~20x gap versus reported earnings - and stockholders' equity jumped by ~$14B in Q4 2017 alone, raising the risk that reported earnings quality/comparability is overstated relative to underlying cash generation.

top_signals: Accounts receivable nearly doubled quarter-over-quarter (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=2024782503.44 to 3781921342.64, 2018-03-31 to 2018-06-30) while revenue grew only ~6% (SalesRevenueGoodsNet=12226272894.36 to 12967141588.15), the most recent data point before the cutoff
Two 10-Q/A amendments for Q1 and Q2 2017 filed simultaneously with the original Q3 2017 10-Q on 2017-11-07, indicating a restatement of previously issued interim financials

FY2017 net income (NetIncomeLoss=21331975819.33) diverged sharply from operating cash flow (NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1022088485.93), a ~20x gap, with a large Q4 2017 earnings spike (NetIncomeLoss=15521393079.56) suggestive of a one-time non-cash item
Allowance for doubtful accounts declined in dollar terms through 2016 (62062298.96 to 38788936.85) and disclosure ceased thereafter even as receivables spiked in 2018

Data comparability is impaired by a 2015 merger event that caused large restatements of the reporting entity's basis (e.g., Assets=44504486692.09 vs Assets=70927510472.67 for essentially the same period-end reported in different filings)
